description: A designed share-card combining a photo and a quoted news excerpt. The top half is a color photograph taken inside an aviation maintenance training facility: a smiling woman (apparently the Labor Secretary) in a white top and clear safety glasses speaks with a female trainee wearing safety glasses and a navy 'AM / AMG' (Aviation Maintenance) t-shirt, with men in business attire and other workers visible in the background under an exposed-beam industrial ceiling. Across the lower portion, over a dark navy panel, is a large white serif/italic headline 'Trade School BOOM!'. Below it, in a rounded white text box, is the 'Business Report' (Greater Baton Rouge Business Report) logo followed by body text: 'Enrollment in two-year vocational programs is now at its highest since record-keeping began in 1992, roughly double the level in 2000, according to a Washington Post analysis of federal data. For many young people, trades offer a path to entrepreneurship, flexibility and independence.' The card uses an upbeat statistic and a friendly hands-on training image to promote the administration's pro-trades/vocational-education and 'American worker' messaging. Target audience: young workers and the general public.
notable_text: Trade School BOOM!; Enrollment in two-year vocational programs is now at its highest since record-keeping began in 1992, roughly double the level in 2000... trades offer a path to entrepreneurship, flexibility and independence.
